Thanks for opening the discussion. Based on the latest market reference of 6.58 and the need to secure committed supply, I can start at 6.72 per unit. That reflects current market conditions while still giving room to move if you are prepared to discuss volume and timing commitments. As the supplier opening the negotiation, I want to anchor near the current observed market level and preserve margin. There is no buyer offer yet, so the appropriate move is to make an initial offer rather than accept or reject.

I can’t move forward at 6.72 per unit. That level is far outside the economics I can support on the buy side, even allowing for current market pressure, and it would not preserve workable downstream margin for us. If you want to continue, the discussion would need to come down materially toward a commercially viable range.
